items.name: Web Search (Brave / Exa)
items.what: Lets your AI search the live internet and read current web pages.
items.why: Out of the box, most AIs only know what they were trained on. This lets them look things up so answers are current and less likely to be made up.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Brave Search or Exa MCP server under Settings → Connectors.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add exa (or brave-search) and paste your API key.

items.name: Fetch (read a URL)
items.what: Give your AI a link and it fetches the page and reads it.
items.why: Perfect for 'summarize this article' or 'pull the key points from this page' without copy-pasting.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add fetch -- uvx mcp-server-fetch
install.claude-desktop: Add the Fetch MCP server in your config file.

items.name: Filesystem
items.what: Lets your AI read and write files in folders you choose on your computer.
items.why: So a chat app can open your documents and save results. (Coding agents like Claude Code and Cursor can already touch files, so this matters most for chat apps like Claude Desktop.)

install.claude-desktop: Add the Filesystem MCP server and list the folders it's allowed to touch.
install.claude-code: Built in — Claude Code already reads/writes files in your project.

items.name: Memory
items.what: Gives your AI a long-term memory that survives between conversations.
items.why: So you don't re-explain your project, preferences, or facts every time. It remembers what matters.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Memory MCP server in your config.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add memory -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory

items.name: Sequential Thinking
items.what: Helps the AI break a hard problem into clear, ordered steps before answering.
items.why: Noticeably better results on tricky, multi-step tasks — planning, debugging, reasoning.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add sequential-thinking -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking

items.name: GitHub
items.what: Lets your AI read and manage your GitHub — code, issues, and pull requests.
items.why: Say 'summarize these open issues' or 'open a PR with this fix' instead of clicking around GitHub yourself.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add github and sign in, or use the official GitHub MCP server.
install.claude-desktop: Add the GitHub connector under Settings → Connectors.
install.cursor: Add the GitHub MCP server to .cursor/mcp.json.

items.name: Context7 (up-to-date docs)
items.what: Feeds your coding agent the latest official documentation for libraries and frameworks.
items.why: Stops the AI from using outdated or made-up code — it pulls the real, current docs for the exact version you use.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add context7 -- npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp

items.name: Playwright (browser control)
items.what: Lets your AI open a real browser to click, type, and take screenshots.
items.why: Great for testing your website, filling forms, or grabbing screenshots — the AI actually drives the browser.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add playwright -- npx -y @playwright/mcp
install.cursor: Add the Playwright MCP server to .cursor/mcp.json.

items.name: PostgreSQL / SQL Database
items.what: Connects your AI to a SQL database so it can run queries and read results.
items.why: Ask questions in plain English ('how many orders last month?') and let the AI write and run the SQL.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add postgres and provide your read-only connection string.
install.claude-desktop: Add the Postgres MCP server with your connection string in config.

items.name: SQLite
items.what: Lets your AI read and query a local SQLite database file.
items.why: A simple, no-server way to let the AI explore structured data on your machine.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add sqlite -- uvx mcp-server-sqlite --db-path ./your.db

items.name: Sentry (error tracking)
items.what: Pulls your app's real error reports into the AI.
items.why: Ask 'what's breaking in production and why?' and get the AI to read the actual stack traces and suggest fixes.

install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add sentry and authorize your Sentry account.

items.name: Docker
items.what: Lets your AI manage Docker containers — build, run, and inspect them.
items.why: Handy for local dev environments and running things in isolation without memorizing Docker commands.

install.claude-code: Add the Docker MCP server per its README.

items.name: Semgrep (security scan)
items.what: Scans your code for security bugs and lets the AI explain and fix them.
items.why: Catches vulnerabilities as you build, in plain language, instead of after something goes wrong.

install.claude-code: Add the Semgrep MCP server and let it scan your repo.

items.name: Project Rules (.cursorrules)
items.what: A file where you tell Cursor how to behave in your project — your coding style, do's and don'ts.
items.why: The AI follows your conventions automatically instead of you correcting it every time.

install.cursor: Create a .cursor/rules folder (or a .cursorrules file) in your project and describe your preferences.

items.name: Agent Instructions (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md)
items.what: A plain-text file your coding agent reads first, describing your project and how to work in it.
items.why: One place to set context and rules — the agent stops guessing and works the way your team does.

install.claude-code: Add a CLAUDE.md at your repo root (run /init to generate one).
install.codex: Add an AGENTS.md file at your repo root.

items.name: Excel / Spreadsheets Skill
items.what: Teaches the AI to read, build, and fix Excel/CSV spreadsheets properly — formulas, formatting, charts.
items.why: Turn a messy CSV into a clean report, or generate a financial model, without wrestling with formulas yourself.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the xlsx Skill in Settings → Capabilities/Skills.
install.claude-code: Install the xlsx skill from the skills marketplace.

items.name: Word Documents Skill
items.what: Teaches the AI to create and edit real Word (.docx) documents — headings, tables, letterheads.
items.why: Get a polished, formatted document (a report, a contract, a memo) instead of plain text you have to reformat.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the docx Skill in Settings.
install.claude-code: Install the docx skill from the skills marketplace.

items.name: Slide Decks (PowerPoint) Skill
items.what: Teaches the AI to build real PowerPoint (.pptx) presentations.
items.why: Describe your deck and get formatted slides back — a huge time-saver for pitches and updates.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the pptx Skill in Settings.
install.claude-code: Install the pptx skill from the skills marketplace.

items.name: PDF Skill
items.what: Teaches the AI to read, fill, merge, split, and create PDF files.
items.why: Pull tables out of a PDF statement, fill a form, or merge documents — no separate PDF tool needed.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the pdf Skill in Settings.
install.claude-code: Install the pdf skill from the skills marketplace.

items.name: Data Analysis / Code Execution
items.what: Lets the AI run real Python in a safe sandbox to analyze data and make charts.
items.why: For exact math and real charts on your data — the AI computes the answer instead of guessing it.

install.claude-desktop: Turn on the Analysis / code tool in Settings.
install.chatgpt: Use the built-in Data Analysis (code interpreter) — just upload a file and ask.

items.name: Stripe (payments)
items.what: Connects your AI to Stripe to look up payments, customers, and revenue.
items.why: Ask about your sales, refunds, or a specific customer in plain English — or have a coding agent wire up Stripe for you.

install.claude-code: Add the Stripe MCP server (Stripe Agent Toolkit) with a restricted API key.
install.claude-desktop: Add the Stripe connector in config with a read-only key.

items.name: Market / Financial Data
items.what: Gives your AI live stock prices, company financials, and market data.
items.why: Ask for a company's numbers or a market snapshot and get real current figures, not stale ones.

install.claude-desktop: Add a financial-data MCP server (e.g. Alpha Vantage or Yahoo Finance) with an API key.

items.name: QuickBooks / Accounting
items.what: Connects your AI to your accounting software to read invoices, expenses, and reports.
items.why: Ask about cash flow, unpaid invoices, or expenses without digging through the accounting app.

install.claude-desktop: Add a QuickBooks MCP connector and authorize your account (read-only recommended).

items.name: BigQuery
items.what: Connects the AI to Google BigQuery to query large datasets.
items.why: Ask business questions across huge tables and let the AI write the SQL and read the results.

install.claude-code: Add a BigQuery MCP server with a service-account key (read-only).

items.name: Snowflake
items.what: Connects the AI to your Snowflake data warehouse.
items.why: Self-serve analytics: ask questions and get answers straight from the warehouse, no analyst queue.

install.claude-code: Add a Snowflake MCP server with read-only credentials.

items.name: Google Sheets
items.what: Lets your AI read and update your Google Sheets.
items.why: Have the AI pull numbers from a sheet, update a tracker, or build a summary tab for you.

install.claude-desktop: Add a Google Sheets / Google Workspace MCP connector and sign in.

items.name: BI Tools (Power BI / Tableau)
items.what: Connects the AI to your BI dashboards and their underlying data.
items.why: Ask what a dashboard is really saying, or generate the query behind a chart, in plain English.

install.claude-desktop: Add the relevant BI MCP connector and authorize it (read-only).

items.name: Figma
items.what: Connects your AI to Figma to read designs and turn them into code or specs.
items.why: Hand the AI a Figma frame and get accurate component code or a clean spec — no more eyeballing pixels.

install.cursor: Enable Figma's Dev Mode MCP server, then add it to .cursor/mcp.json.
install.claude-code: Add the Figma MCP server and connect your account.

items.name: shadcn/ui components
items.what: Lets your coding agent browse and add polished, ready-made UI components.
items.why: Build good-looking interfaces fast using proven components instead of styling everything from scratch.

install.cursor: Add the shadcn MCP server to .cursor/mcp.json.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add shadcn -- npx -y shadcn@latest mcp

items.name: v0 (UI from a prompt)
items.what: Describe a screen and get a working UI mockup and code back.
items.why: Go from idea to a real, editable interface in seconds — great for prototyping.

install.chatgpt: Use v0.app in your browser (by Vercel); no coding setup needed.

items.name: Image Generation
items.what: Create and edit images from a text description.
items.why: Make logos, mockups, social graphics, or edits without opening a design tool.

install.chatgpt: Built in — just ask ChatGPT to create or edit an image.
install.gemini: Built in — ask Gemini to generate an image.

items.name: Canva
items.what: Connects your AI to Canva to create and edit designs.
items.why: Generate on-brand graphics, social posts, and presentations through a chat instead of designing each by hand.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Canva connector under Settings → Connectors and sign in.

items.name: YouTube Transcripts
items.what: Pulls the transcript of a YouTube video so the AI can read it.
items.why: Summarize a video, pull quotes, or repurpose it into a blog post or script without watching it all.

install.claude-desktop: Add a YouTube transcript MCP server in your config.

items.name: AI Video Generation
items.what: Create short video clips from a text prompt or an image.
items.why: Produce b-roll, ads, or concept clips without a camera or an editor.

install.gemini: Use Google's video generation (Veo) where available in the app.
install.chatgpt: Use OpenAI's video generation (Sora) where available.

items.name: Jira & Confluence (Atlassian)
items.what: Connects your AI to Jira issues and Confluence pages.
items.why: Create tickets, summarize a sprint, or draft a spec page straight from a conversation.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Atlassian connector under Settings → Connectors and sign in.
install.claude-code: Add the Atlassian MCP server and authorize it.

items.name: Notion
items.what: Connects your AI to your Notion workspace to read and write pages.
items.why: Turn a chat into a Notion doc, update a tracker, or pull notes into your work automatically.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Notion connector under Settings → Connectors and sign in.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add notion and authorize your workspace.

items.name: Linear
items.what: Connects your AI to Linear to manage issues and projects.
items.why: Create and update issues, or get a status summary, without leaving the conversation.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Linear connector under Settings → Connectors.
install.claude-code: Add the Linear MCP server and authorize it.

items.name: Slack
items.what: Connects your AI to Slack to read and send messages.
items.why: Summarize a busy channel, draft an announcement, or find that decision buried in threads.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Slack connector under Settings → Connectors and authorize your workspace.

items.name: Google Analytics
items.what: Connects your AI to your website analytics.
items.why: Ask 'where did traffic come from last week?' and get a real answer instead of clicking through reports.

install.claude-desktop: Add a Google Analytics MCP connector and authorize it (read-only).

items.name: Apify (web scraping)
items.what: Lets your AI run ready-made web scrapers to pull data from sites.
items.why: Collect data from social media, maps, e-commerce, or search — without writing a scraper yourself.

install.claude-desktop: Add the Apify MCP server with your Apify token.
install.claude-code: Run: claude mcp add apify and provide your token.

items.name: SEO Data (DataForSEO / Ahrefs)
items.what: Gives your AI keyword, ranking, and backlink data.
items.why: Do keyword research and competitor checks in a chat, then draft content around what you find.

install.claude-desktop: Add an SEO data MCP server (e.g. DataForSEO) with an API key.

items.name: GDPR Compliance Skill
items.what: Teaches the AI to answer GDPR questions and draft privacy documents with real article references.
items.why: Get grounded, citable privacy guidance and first-draft policies instead of vague generic answers.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the GDPR compliance Skill.
install.claude-code: Install the gdpr-compliance skill from a skills marketplace.

items.name: India DPDPA Compliance Skill
items.what: Teaches the AI India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act — obligations, notices, and breach rules.
items.why: Practical, India-specific privacy help and document drafts, grounded in the actual Act and Rules.

install.claude-code: Install the dpdpa skill from a skills marketplace.
install.claude-desktop: Enable the DPDPA compliance Skill.

items.name: Contract Review Skill
items.what: Teaches the AI to read a contract, flag risky clauses, and explain them plainly.
items.why: A fast first pass on an agreement — what to watch for — before it reaches a lawyer.

install.claude-desktop: Enable a contract-review Skill (pair it with the PDF skill for scanned files).

items.name: HIPAA Compliance Skill
items.what: Teaches the AI US health-privacy rules (HIPAA) and how to handle protected health information.
items.why: Guidance and document drafts that respect health-data rules, instead of risky generic advice.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the HIPAA compliance Skill.
install.claude-code: Install the hipaa-compliance skill from a skills marketplace.

items.name: PubMed (medical research)
items.what: Connects your AI to PubMed to search medical literature.
items.why: Find and summarize real studies with citations instead of relying on the model's memory.

install.claude-desktop: Add a PubMed MCP server in your config.

items.name: Claude Code Plugins
items.what: Installable bundles that add a set of skills, slash-commands, and connectors to Claude Code at once.
items.why: Instead of wiring things up one by one, install a ready-made bundle (for code review, memory, and more) with a single command.

install.claude-code: Run: /plugin marketplace add <owner/repo>, then /plugin install <name>.

items.name: Custom GPTs
items.what: Custom versions of ChatGPT that bundle instructions, knowledge, and actions for a specific job.
items.why: Set up a reusable assistant once (e.g. 'my brand-voice writer') and everyone can use it — no prompt engineering each time.

install.chatgpt: In ChatGPT, open the GPT store or 'Create a GPT' — no coding needed.

items.name: Superpowers
items.what: A framework that gives Claude Code a big library of expert 'skills' plus a disciplined workflow (planning, testing, code review).
items.why: One install turns Claude Code into a far more capable, methodical developer instead of a one-off helper.

install.claude-code: Run: /plugin marketplace add obra/superpowers, then /plugin install superpowers

items.name: Everything Claude Code (ECC)
items.what: A power-user setup for coding agents: bundled skills, memory, security checks, and research-first habits.
items.why: A tuned configuration that makes agents faster and more reliable on real projects — for people past the basics.

install.claude-code: Follow the repo's install script to apply the ECC configuration.

items.name: claude-mem (persistent memory)
items.what: Gives your coding agent a memory across sessions by capturing and compressing what it did.
items.why: Stop re-explaining your project every session — the agent remembers past work and decisions.

install.claude-code: Run: npx claude-mem install (see the repo README).

items.name: Caveman (token saver)
items.what: Makes the AI reply in an ultra-compressed 'caveman' style to save tokens (and money).
items.why: Cuts token use by roughly 65% on long sessions with no loss of technical accuracy.

install.claude-code: Run: /plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman, then install the caveman skill.

items.name: gstack (startup-team setup)
items.what: A bundle of 23 opinionated tools that act like a startup team — CEO, designer, eng manager, and QA roles.
items.why: A ready-made 'team in a box' for building products with Claude Code, based on a well-known founder's setup.

install.claude-code: Follow the gstack repo instructions to install the tool bundle.

items.name: Graphify (code/docs knowledge graph)
items.what: Turns a folder of code, SQL schemas, or docs into a queryable knowledge graph the AI can explore.
items.why: Ask questions across a whole codebase or dataset at once, instead of file by file.

install.claude-code: Install the graphify skill from its repo, then point it at a folder.

items.name: Task Master
items.what: An AI task-management system that breaks a project into tasks your agent works through one by one.
items.why: Keeps big builds organized and on-track so the agent ships step by step instead of wandering.

install.claude-code: Run: npm i -g task-master-ai (see repo), then initialize it in your project.

items.name: Sub-agents collection
items.what: A marketplace of specialized sub-agents (code reviewer, debugger, architect) for Claude Code.
items.why: Drop in expert helpers for specific jobs instead of relying on one generalist agent.

install.claude-code: Run: /plugin marketplace add wshobson/agents, then install the agents you want.

items.name: Claude Code Templates
items.what: A CLI that sets up and monitors Claude Code with ready-made configs and dashboards.
items.why: Skip manual setup — get a solid Claude Code configuration and health view in one command.

install.claude-code: Run: npx claude-code-templates@latest

items.name: Design-Review Workflows
items.what: Battle-tested Claude Code workflows and configs, including strong automated design-review passes.
items.why: Copy proven setups (like a rigorous UI review) instead of inventing your own from scratch.

install.claude-code: Copy the workflow/config files from the repo into your project.

items.name: cc-switch (agent manager)
items.what: A desktop app to manage and switch between Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode setups in one place.
items.why: Handy if you use more than one coding agent and want a single control panel.

install.claude-code: Download the cc-switch desktop app from the repo releases.

items.name: Hermes Agent
items.what: An open agent framework designed to adapt and improve as you work with it.
items.why: A widely-starred community framework for building agents that get better over time.

install.claude-code: Follow the hermes-agent repo setup instructions.

items.name: Higgsfield
items.what: Turn a photo or prompt into cinematic AI video with viral camera presets.
items.why: Make scroll-stopping social clips, ads, and product videos with no camera or editor.

install.standalone: Use it at https://higgsfield.ai — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Runway
items.what: Pro-grade AI video generation and editing (Gen-4.5).
items.why: The highest-quality AI video with strong motion control — the pro's pick.

install.standalone: Use it at https://runwayml.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Kling AI
items.what: AI video with long, consistent, cinematic shots and native audio.
items.why: Keeps faces and products consistent across longer takes.

install.standalone: Use it at https://klingai.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Pika
items.what: Fast AI video with lip-sync for talking characters.
items.why: Best when a character actually needs to speak.

install.standalone: Use it at https://pika.art — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Luma Dream Machine
items.what: Animate a still image into video with believable camera motion.
items.why: Dreamy image-to-video for music videos, mood pieces, and brand content.

install.standalone: Use it at https://lumalabs.ai/dream-machine — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: HeyGen
items.what: Create talking-avatar and spokesperson videos in many languages.
items.why: Training and marketing videos without filming a real presenter.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.heygen.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Descript
items.what: Edit video and podcasts by editing the transcript, with AI cleanup.
items.why: Cut filler words and edit media as easily as a text document.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.descript.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: ElevenLabs
items.what: Lifelike AI voices and voice cloning for narration and dubbing.
items.why: Add professional voiceover or dub content into other languages.

install.standalone: Use it at https://elevenlabs.io — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Suno
items.what: Generate full songs with vocals from a text prompt.
items.why: Original background music or jingles in seconds.

install.standalone: Use it at https://suno.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: CapCut
items.what: Popular video editor with AI captions, effects, and background cutouts.
items.why: Fast social-video editing with AI helpers, free to start.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.capcut.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Midjourney
items.what: Best-in-class AI image generation with a distinctive artistic look.
items.why: The go-to for high-quality concept art and striking visuals.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.midjourney.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Ideogram
items.what: AI images that render readable text well — posters, logos, typography.
items.why: Best when your image needs actual words in it.

install.standalone: Use it at https://ideogram.ai — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Adobe Firefly
items.what: Adobe's commercially-safe AI image and design generator.
items.why: Trained on licensed content — safer for commercial work.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.adobe.com/products/firefly.html — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Leonardo AI
items.what: AI image generation tuned for game art, assets, and concepts.
items.why: Fine control and specialized models for production art.

install.standalone: Use it at https://leonardo.ai — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Krea
items.what: Real-time AI image generation and enhancement canvas.
items.why: Sketch and steer images live — great for fast ideation.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.krea.ai — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Recraft
items.what: AI for vector art, icons, and on-brand design systems.
items.why: Generate editable vectors and consistent brand assets.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.recraft.ai — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Freepik AI
items.what: A suite of low-cost AI image/video tools plus a huge asset library.
items.why: Cheap generation bundled with stock assets.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.freepik.com/ai — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Magnific
items.what: AI upscaler and enhancer that adds realistic detail.
items.why: Turn small or soft images into crisp, detailed ones.

install.standalone: Use it at https://magnific.ai — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Photoroom
items.what: AI product photos — remove backgrounds and create studio shots.
items.why: Ecommerce-ready product images without a photo studio.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.photoroom.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Gamma
items.what: Generate polished slide decks, docs, and simple websites from a prompt.
items.why: Beautiful presentations and one-pagers in minutes.

install.standalone: Use it at https://gamma.app — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Spellbook
items.what: AI contract drafting and redlining right inside Microsoft Word.
items.why: Review and mark up contracts far faster, where lawyers already work.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.spellbook.legal — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: LegalOn
items.what: Automated contract review with attorney-built playbooks.
items.why: Flags risky clauses and suggests fixes against best-practice standards.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.legalontech.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Lexis+ AI
items.what: Legal research grounded in real case law with citations and validation.
items.why: Verifiable research that avoids the fake citations general chatbots invent.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.lexisnexis.com/en-us/products/lexis-plus-ai.page — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: CoCounsel
items.what: Legal AI built on Westlaw and Practical Law.
items.why: Trusted research, drafting, and review on authoritative sources.

install.standalone: Use it at https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/en/c/cocounsel — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: Harvey
items.what: Enterprise legal AI for large firms and in-house teams.
items.why: A domain-trained assistant for complex legal work at scale.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.harvey.ai — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: GC AI
items.what: AI platform for in-house/corporate legal — research, contracts, daily chat.
items.why: One tool for a corporate legal team's everyday work.

install.standalone: Use it at https://gc.ai — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: TheLawGPT
items.what: Accessible AI contract review for solo lawyers and small firms.
items.why: Understand a contract a client just emailed you, quickly and cheaply.

install.standalone: Use it at https://www.thelawgpt.com — sign up in your browser; nothing to install into another AI.

items.name: SOC 2 Compliance Skill
items.what: Guides SOC 2 readiness — Trust Services Criteria, controls, and evidence.
items.why: Turns a scary audit into a grounded checklist and first-draft policies.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the skill in Settings → Skills.
install.claude-code: Install it from a skills marketplace (e.g. /plugin install).

items.name: ISO 27001 Compliance Skill
items.what: Helps build an information-security management system to ISO 27001.
items.why: Structured guidance for the world's main infosec certification.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the skill in Settings → Skills.
install.claude-code: Install it from a skills marketplace (e.g. /plugin install).

items.name: ISO 27701 Privacy Skill
items.what: Extends ISO 27001 to privacy information management (PIMS).
items.why: For teams adding privacy controls on top of security.

install.claude-desktop: Enable the skill in Settings → Skills.

roles.label: Must-have for everyone
roles.description: Start here. These work for almost any job and are the best first things to set up.

roles.label: Software Developer
roles.description: Write, review, test, and ship code faster with the right connectors and rules.

roles.label: Finance & Accounting
roles.description: Spreadsheets, reports, statements, and financial data — with far less manual work.

roles.label: Data Analytics
roles.description: Query databases, crunch CSVs, and turn raw data into answers and charts.

roles.label: UI / UX Designer
roles.description: Turn ideas into mockups and components, and pull designs into code.

roles.label: Legal & Compliance
roles.description: Draft policies, review contracts, and check work against real regulations.

roles.label: Healthcare
roles.description: Research, documentation, and privacy-aware help for clinical and health work.

roles.label: Business Intelligence
roles.description: Connect to your BI stack and warehouse to answer business questions.

roles.label: Business Analyst
roles.description: Requirements, tickets, docs, and stakeholder-ready summaries.

roles.label: Graphic Designer
roles.description: Generate and edit images, and speed up creative production.

roles.label: Video Creator
roles.description: Scripts, transcripts, editing help, and AI video generation.

roles.label: Project Management
roles.description: Keep tasks, docs, and teams in sync across your tools.

roles.label: Marketing
roles.description: Content, SEO, analytics, and research — end to end.

types.label: Skill
types.description: A packaged set of instructions that teaches your AI a specialized task — like writing a legal privacy notice, building a slide deck, or cleaning a spreadsheet. You 'install' it and then just ask for the task.

types.label: Connector (MCP)
types.description: A bridge that lets your AI reach an outside app or data source — GitHub, Gmail, a database, Slack. Most use a standard called MCP (Model Context Protocol). Think of it as giving your AI a new sense or a new hand.

types.label: Plugin
types.description: A bundle you install into a tool that adds a set of features at once — often several skills, commands, and connectors packaged together.

types.label: Rule / Config
types.description: A short instructions file that shapes how a coding agent behaves in your project — like a house-style guide the AI reads before it works (e.g. .cursorrules, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md).

types.label: Extension
types.description: An add-on you install into an app or code editor (like a VS Code or Cursor extension) to add AI features to that app.

types.label: App
types.description: A standalone AI product you use on its own — its own website or app (like an AI video maker or a data-analysis tool). You don't install it into another AI; you just sign up and use it.

surfaces.label: Chat
surfaces.description: Works in AI chat apps like Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, or Gemini — the kind where you type a message and it replies.

surfaces.label: Code
surfaces.description: Works in AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor — tools that can read and edit your files and run commands for you.

surfaces.label: Both
surfaces.description: Works in both chat apps and coding agents.

surfaces.label: Standalone
surfaces.description: Used on its own — it has its own website or app, and doesn't run inside Claude, ChatGPT, or Cursor.

costs.label: Free
costs.description: Free to use (open-source or a genuinely free product). No card needed to get real value.

costs.label: Freemium
costs.description: Free tier to start, with paid plans for heavier use or advanced features.

costs.label: Paid
costs.description: Needs a paid subscription (usually a modest monthly fee).

costs.label: Enterprise
costs.description: Sold to teams/companies, often with sales-led or premium pricing. Powerful, but not a quick solo sign-up.

collections.name: Anthropic Skills (official)
collections.what: Anthropic's own public collection of Agent Skills — the source of the built-in document skills (Word, PDF, Excel, PowerPoint) and more.

collections.name: Awesome Claude Skills (Composio)
collections.what: A well-organized, frequently-referenced curated list of Claude skills and integrations by category.

collections.name: Awesome Claude Code
collections.what: A big curated list of skills, hooks, slash-commands, and configs for Claude Code.

collections.name: Awesome Agent Skills (VoltAgent)
collections.what: 1000+ agent skills from official teams and the community, portable across Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, and more.

collections.name: claude-skills (337 skills)
collections.what: A large open library — 330+ skills, 30+ agents, 70+ commands — spanning engineering, marketing, finance, compliance, and more.

collections.name: Awesome Claude Skills (travisvn)
collections.what: Another curated list of Claude skills, resources, and tools for customizing Claude workflows.

collections.name: aiskill.market
collections.what: A browsable marketplace of skills, plugins, and commands with install counts (thousands of publishers).

collections.name: Claude Marketplaces
collections.what: A community-curated directory of Claude Code skills, plugins, and MCP servers, updated daily from GitHub.

collections.name: ClaudeSkills.info
collections.what: A browsable library of free, open-source Claude Code skills and agent skills.

collections.name: LobeHub Skills
collections.what: A polished skills marketplace for Claude, Codex, and ChatGPT within the LobeHub ecosystem.

collections.name: MCP Market — Skills
collections.what: A directory of Agent Skills for Claude.ai, Claude Code, Codex, and ChatGPT, alongside MCP servers.

collections.name: skills.sh
collections.what: An npm-style installer for skills — one command adds a skill across Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Cursor.

collections.name: SkillsLLM
collections.what: A directory of AI skills and agents by category, linking through to their GitHub repos.

collections.name: SkillsMP
collections.what: A huge searchable index of SKILL.md files scraped from public GitHub (millions indexed). Great for finding a specific skill; audit before installing.
